world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: distance measurement units change (meters vs yards displayed on UI)
course layouts and hole designs vary; unused object sprites suggest environmental variation between holes

environmental_storytelling.example: different hole layouts communicate progression and challenge escalation without narrative
meters vs yards display adapts visual UI to regional context, affecting how player reads distance feedback

notes: Golf (NES/Famicom) is a minimal-narrative sports title with no faction systems, danger states, or political elements. Primary visual communication occurs through course design and UI localization. Unused graphics suggest cut environmental variety. The presence of Mario (noted in source) likely serves as iconic branding rather than narrative role. As a pure sports simulation, world-state communication is limited to game progression through hole sequences and measurement feedback.
sources_used: tcrf - Golf (NES, Famicom Disk System) - The Cutting Room Floor
extraction_notes: Source material focuses on technical data (unused assets, regional differences, platform history) rather than visual communication design. No documentation of danger signals, faction identity, environmental storytelling techniques beyond course layout, or behavioral manifestation systems. Game appears incompatible with Two Fires manifestation layer model due to lack of world-state complexity, faction systems, or danger/safety signaling mechanics.
